questions.question: Eugene, who weighs $450\text{ N}$, runs up ten steps. Each step is $0.20\text{ m}$ high. If he takes five seconds to reach the top, what is his power output?
questions.explanation:
Total height $h = 10 \times 0.20 = 2.0\text{ m}$. Work $W = F \times h = 450 \times 2.0 = 900\text{ J}$. Power $P = 900 / 5 = 180\text{ W}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using only the height of a single step ($0.20\text{ m}$) in the calculation ($18\text{ W}$).

questions.question: A $40\text{ W}$ filament lamp converts $10\%$ of its electrical energy into light energy. How much light energy is produced in five minutes?
questions.explanation:
Total energy $E = P \times t = 40\text{ W} \times (5 \times 60)\text{ s} = 12000\text{ J}$. Light energy $= 10\% \text{ of } 12000 = 1200\text{ J} = 1.2\text{ kJ}$. 

**Common mistake:** Forgetting to convert minutes to seconds or failing to apply the $10\%$ efficiency factor.

questions.question: Data for a power station shows a daily useful energy output of $7.5 \times 10^{13}\text{ J}$. Calculate the average power generated by the station in megawatts ($MW$).
questions.explanation:
Time in one day $= 24 \times 3600 = 86400\text{ s}$. Power $= (7.5 \times 10^{13}) / 86400 \approx 868055556\text{ W} \approx 868\text{ MW}$. 

**Common mistake:** Dividing by $24$ (hours) only rather than seconds.

questions.question: A machine with an efficiency of $25\%$ is used to lift a $200\text{ kg}$ load to a height of $30\text{ m}$. If the total power input is $24\text{ kW}$, how long does the lift take? (Take $g = 10\text{ N/kg}$)
questions.explanation:
Useful Power $= 25\% \text{ of } 24000\text{ W} = 6000\text{ W}$. Useful Work $W = mgh = 200 \times 10 \times 30 = 60000\text{ J}$. Time $t = W / P_{useful} = 60000 / 6000 = 10\text{ s}$. 

**Common mistake:** Using the total input power ($24\text{ kW}$) instead of the useful power output.

questions.question: A toy wheel is launched by pushing an adapter vertically upwards along a rod. In Experiment A, the full length of the rod is used. In Experiment B, only half the length is used. If the adapter is pushed with the same force over the same time in both, which statement is correct?
options: The launching power in A is twice that of B
The launching power in B is twice that of A
The launching power is the same in both
No work is done in either case
questions.explanation:
In A, the distance $s$ is doubled compared to B. Since Work $= Fs$, Work in A is double. Because Power $=$ Work $/$ time and time is the same, Power in A is double. 

**Common mistake:** Thinking power only depends on the force applied regardless of distance.
